howto_title: How to use YouTube Playlist Length
howto_step1_title: Paste a playlist or video link
howto_step1_text: Copy any YouTube playlist URL or video URL and paste it into the text box above.
howto_step2_text: Optionally limit the video range, set a custom playback speed, or enter how many videos you've already watched.
howto_step3_text: Instantly see total duration at multiple speeds, plus progress tracking and a personalized watch schedule.
faq_q1: How do I find the total length of a YouTube playlist?
faq_a1: Paste the playlist URL into the box above and click "Analyze Playlist". The tool calls the YouTube API to sum up every video's duration and shows you the total, plus times at 1.25x, 1.5x, 1.75x, and 2x speed.
faq_q2: Can I see how long specific videos in a playlist will take?
faq_a2: Yes — use the Range Start and Range End fields to select a subset of videos (e.g., videos 10–50). The total duration will reflect only that range.
faq_q3: Does this work with private playlists?
faq_a3: The tool uses the public YouTube Data API. Private playlists are not accessible without authentication. Unlisted playlists will work as long as you have the direct link.
faq_a4: The durations come directly from YouTube's API, so they are exactly as accurate as what YouTube reports. Unavailable or deleted videos are excluded from the total.
faq_q5: Can I use my own YouTube API key?
faq_a5: Yes — enter your API key in the "YouTube API key" field. This is useful if you hit quota limits or want to make requests on your own quota.
faq_q6: How does the Watch Schedule calculator work?
faq_a6: After analyzing a playlist, scroll to the Watch Schedule card. Enter how many hours you watch per day, select which days of the week you watch, and it will calculate the finish date accounting for skipped days — including a circular progress indicator if you enter how many videos you've already watched.
why_text: Whether you're planning to binge a full YouTube course, estimate how long a podcast playlist will take, or track your progress through a lecture series — this free tool gives you accurate watch-time estimates in seconds. No sign-up, no ads blocker needed, no data stored. Just paste, click, and know exactly when you'll finish.
thumb_howto_title: How to download a YouTube thumbnail
thumb_howto_step1_text: Right-click a video and choose "Copy video URL", or copy any YouTube link — watch URLs, youtu.be short links, embed URLs and shorts links all work.
thumb_howto_step2_title: Paste it into the box above
thumb_howto_step2_text: The tool parses the video ID from any URL format and requests every thumbnail size YouTube publishes for that video.
thumb_howto_step3_title: Download any of the four sizes
thumb_howto_step3_text: Click a thumbnail to open it full size in a new tab, then right-click and choose "Save image as…" to keep the original JPEG.
thumb_why_text: Creators making reaction videos, designers building mockups, bloggers writing video round-ups, and students saving reference material all need clean thumbnail assets. This free tool gives you every size YouTube exposes — from max resolution 1280×720 down to 120×90 — in one click, with no watermark, no login and no software to install.
thumb_faq_q1: What thumbnail sizes does this tool support?
thumb_faq_a1: All four sizes YouTube publishes: max resolution (1280×720), high quality (480×360), medium quality (320×180) and default (120×90). Some older or private videos may not have every size available.
thumb_faq_q2: Is it legal to download a YouTube thumbnail?
thumb_faq_a2: Downloading a thumbnail is technically the same as viewing a webpage — but re-using someone else's thumbnail commercially can infringe their copyright. Personal reference, fair-use commentary and reaction content are generally treated the same as embedded video content. Always credit the original creator.
thumb_faq_q3: Why do some videos not have a max-resolution thumbnail?
thumb_faq_a3: YouTube only generates maxresdefault.jpg (1280×720) for videos uploaded in HD. Older or low-resolution uploads may only expose the medium and default sizes.
thumb_faq_q4: Can I download the animated "hover" preview?
thumb_faq_a4: No — YouTube's animated previews are WebP storyboards served through a different endpoint, not standard image files. This tool works with the four static JPEG thumbnails only.
thumb_faq_q5: Do I need an API key or an account?
thumb_faq_a5: No. Thumbnails are served publicly by YouTube's i.ytimg.com CDN and require no authentication or API quota.
thumb_faq_q6: Do you store the URLs I paste?
thumb_faq_a6: No. The URL you paste is parsed on the server only long enough to extract the video ID; the thumbnails themselves are served directly by YouTube's CDN and never touch our servers. Nothing is logged or retained.
ts_howto_title: How to create a YouTube timestamp link
ts_howto_step1_text: Any YouTube URL format works: watch links, youtu.be short links, embed URLs, or even URLs that already contain a timestamp — the generator normalizes them.
ts_howto_step2_text: Fill in hours, minutes and seconds where you want the video to start playing. Empty fields count as zero, so for "1 min 30 sec in" you only need 1 in Minutes and 30 in Seconds.
ts_howto_step3_title: Copy and share the generated link
ts_howto_step3_text: Click Copy to grab the link. Anyone who opens it — on desktop, mobile web, or the official YouTube app — will start watching at the exact timestamp.
ts_why_title: Why use YouTube Timestamp Link Generator?
ts_why_text: Sharing a video that "starts at the good part" removes friction and respects your audience's time. Whether you're sending a friend to a joke, a student to an equation, a teammate to a design demo, or a listener to the answer they asked about — a timestamped link is the difference between "watch this whole thing" and "here's exactly what I meant". This generator produces the exact URL YouTube expects, so it works everywhere: browsers, mobile apps, embeds and search snippets.
ts_faq_q1: How does a YouTube timestamp link actually work?
ts_faq_a1: YouTube's watch URL accepts a t query parameter with a value in seconds — for example, ?t=90 starts a video at 1:30. This tool converts your entered hours, minutes and seconds into that seconds value automatically.
ts_faq_q2: Do timestamped links work on mobile and in the YouTube app?
ts_faq_a2: Yes. Desktop web, mobile web, and the official YouTube iOS and Android apps all honor the t parameter. Embedded players use a separate start parameter, and you can convert between the two easily.
ts_faq_q3: Can I use a youtu.be short link?
ts_faq_a3: Yes. Paste any YouTube URL format — watch, youtu.be, embed, shorts — and the tool converts it to the canonical shareable form https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=…&t=…s.
ts_faq_q4: How do I make an embedded video start at a specific time?
ts_faq_a4: For an <iframe> embed, use the /embed/ URL and pass start=SECONDS. Example: https://www.youtube.com/embed/dQw4w9WgXcQ?start=90 starts 1:30 in.
ts_faq_q5: Why does my browser sometimes ignore the timestamp?
ts_faq_a5: A few common reasons: (1) an ad is still playing — the timestamp only takes effect once the actual video begins; (2) you were already on that video and the URL fragment doesn't trigger a reload; (3) the video was re-uploaded or trimmed and the timestamp now exceeds its duration.
ts_faq_q6: Can I create a link that starts AND ends at specific times?
ts_faq_a6: Not on a standard YouTube watch link — YouTube doesn't expose an "end" parameter for viewers. Embedded players support both start and end parameters. For sharing, third-party wrappers like sharefromhere.com let you set both boundaries.
